message:
🐛 fix(watchdog): return JSON 503 for XHR/fetch calls, not HTML (#8407)

The watchdog's serveFallback used Accept alone to decide between HTML and
JSON responses. Browser `fetch()` defaults Accept to `*/*`, which matched
the HTML branch — so a cached page whose `fetch('/api/auth/login')` hit
the watchdog while the backend was still booting got an HTML body. The
client JS then called `response.json()`, which threw, and the login
button silently did nothing. The user had to hit reload to land on the
actual watchdog page.

Introduce isAPIRequest() and consult it first: any request under /api/,
/ws/, or /sse/, any non-GET/HEAD method, or an explicit
`Accept: application/json` now always gets the 503 JSON body regardless
of the Accept header. Top-level HTML navigations (GET /, GET /login,
etc.) still render the branded reconnecting page as before.

Adds a table-driven test covering both branches — the specific cases
that triggered the original bug (POST /api/auth/login with */*), the
WebSocket/SSE paths, and the navigation paths that must keep getting
HTML.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
